hint: Someone who saw what happened that night at the pier.
story: The precinct phone rang at 2 AM. A dock worker had vanished from Vermillion Pier. No body, no note — just a pair of boots at the water's edge. Someone had seen something…
resultTeaser: A witness has come forward. But can their testimony be trusted? The trail leads deeper…

hint: A sheltered place along the coast where ships dock and secrets hide.
story: The witness spoke in whispers. She described a figure in a long coat, dragging something heavy toward the old harbour warehouse. The fog was thick that night…
resultTeaser: The harbour warehouse holds more than cargo. Something was left behind in the dust…

hint: A type of firearm with a rotating cylinder — cold steel under a tarp.
story: Inside the warehouse, dust covered everything — except a cleared circle on the floor. And there, half-hidden beneath a tarp: cold steel. Someone had left behind their weapon…
resultTeaser: The weapon has a serial number. It can be traced. The net is closing…

hint: The name on the pawn shop receipt — a regular who always paid in cash.
story: The serial number traced back to a pawn shop on 5th Avenue. The owner's records showed one name — a regular who always paid in cash and never gave a straight answer…
resultTeaser: You have a name. Now you need proof. One final piece to seal the case…

hint: The final word the jury delivered — the conclusion of justice.
story: The evidence was laid out on the desk. The coat fibers, the weapon, the witness testimony — it all pointed to one person. The jury needed only 30 minutes. The verdict was clear…
resultTeaser: Justice has been served. The Vermillion Pier case is closed. Outstanding work, Detective.
